evidence_rea: SHALL process personal data such as: the unique User ID set by Google Analytics, the date and time, the title of the page being viewed, the URL of the page being viewed, the URL of the page that was viewed prior to the current page, the screen resolution, the time in local timezone, the files that were clicked on and downloaded, the links clicked on to an outside domain, the type of device, and the country, region, and city.
evidence_reg: The Provider SHALL not process personal data revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious or philosophical beliefs, or trade union membership, and the processing of genetic data, biometric data for the purpose of uniquely identifying a natural person, data concerning health or data concerning a natural person's sex life or sexual orientation.
per_reg_comparisons.reasoning_short: The REA lists analytics-related data, while the REG prohibits special-category data; there is no quote-supported statement that the REA processes the prohibited categories, so no direct conflict is established.

evidence_rea: This minimal build SHALL process personal data
evidence_reg: Processing shall be lawful only if and to the extent that at least one of the following applies: (a) the data subject has given consent to the processing of his or her personal data for one or more specific purposes;
per_reg_comparisons.reasoning_short: The REA mandates processing certain data, while the REG states a lawfulness condition based on consent; the REA does not address lawfulness or deny consent, so omission alone is not a deviation.

evidence_rea: This minimal build SHALL process personal data such as: the unique User ID set by Google Analytics, the date and time, the title of the page being viewed, the URL of the page being viewed, the URL of the page that was viewed prior to the current page, the screen resolution, the time in local timezone, the files that were clicked on and downloaded, the links clicked on to an outside domain, the type of device, and the country, region, and city.
evidence_reg: The Controller SHALL provide the data subject with the following further information necessary to ensure fair and transparent processing, at the time when personal data are obtained: (a) the period for which the personal data will be stored, or if that is not possible, the criteria used to determine that period;
per_reg_comparisons.reasoning_short: The REA concerns processing categories of data, while the REG concerns informing the data subject about storage period when data are obtained; they do not express the same or directly conflicting constraint.
